perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall: (i) Refrain from disclosing any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to third parties for two (2) years following the date that Disclosing Party first discloses such Confidential Information to Receiving Party, except as expressly provided in Sections 2(b) and 2(c) of this Agreement; (ii) Take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, to keep confidential the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party; (iii) Refrain from disclosing, reproducing, summarizing and/or distributing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party except in pursuance of Receiving Party's business relationship with Disclosing Party, and only as otherwise provided hereunder; and (iv) Refrain from reverse engineering, decompiling or disassembling any software code and/or pre-release hardware devices disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party under the terms of this Agreement, except as expressly permitted by applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall: (i) Refrain from disclosing any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to third parties for two (2) years following the date that Disclosing Party first discloses such Confidential Information to Receiving Party, except as expressly provided in Sections 2(b) and 2(c) of this Agreement; (ii) Take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, to keep confidential the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party; (iii) Refrain from disclosing, reproducing, summarizing and/or distributing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party except in pursuance of Receiving Party's business relationship with Disclosing Party, and only as otherwise provided hereunder; and (iv) **Unless the Disclosing Party agrees otherwise in writing, Receiving Party may reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any software code and/or pre-release hardware devices disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party under the terms of this Agreement, even if not expressly permitted by applicable law.**
perturbation.explanation: The added clause directly contradicts the original statement in section 2(a)(iv). The original text prohibits reverse engineering unless permitted by law, but the modified text allows it if the Disclosing Party agrees, even if the law prohibits it. This introduces uncertainty as to whether legal restrictions apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall: (i) Refrain from disclosing any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to third parties for two (2) years following the date that Disclosing Party first discloses such Confidential Information to Receiving Party, except as expressly provided in Sections 2(b) and 2(c) of this Agreement; (ii) Take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, to keep confidential the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party; (iii) Refrain from disclosing, reproducing, summarizing and/or distributing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party except in pursuance of Receiving Party's business relationship with Disclosing Party, and only as otherwise provided hereunder; and (iv) Unless the Disclosing Party agrees otherwise in writing, Receiving Party may reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any software code and/or pre-release hardware devices disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party under the terms of this Agreement, even if not expressly permitted by applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 2(a)(iv) prohibits reverse engineering unless permitted by law. The changed text allows reverse engineering if the Disclosing Party agrees, 'even if not expressly permitted by applicable law.' This directly contradicts the original restriction and introduces uncertainty regarding legal restrictions, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which is the same as the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall notify the undersigned Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party and its employees and consultants, and will cooperate with Disclosing Party in every reasonable way to help Disclosing Party regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall notify the undersigned Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party and its employees and consultants, **but such notification is only required if the unauthorized use or disclosure results in direct financial loss to the Disclosing Party, proven by clear evidence.** and will cooperate with Disclosing Party in every reasonable way to help Disclosing Party regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change imposes a condition on the notification requirement. Originally, any unauthorized use triggers notification. Now, it only triggers if there's direct financial loss with clear evidence. This contradicts the original intent and definition of Confidential Information, where any unauthorized disclosure is a concern, not just those with immediate, provable financial loss.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall notify the undersigned Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party and its employees and consultants, but such notification is only required if the unauthorized use or disclosure results in direct financial loss to the Disclosing Party, proven by clear evidence. and will cooperate with Disclosing Party in every reasonable way to help Disclosing Party regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition (direct financial loss proven by clear evidence) to the notification requirement for unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the original intent that *any* unauthorized use or disclosure should trigger notification. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text in this instance, highlights the contradiction because it includes the added condition, which limits the scope of the original notification obligation, creating a conflict within the document, based on the explanation given.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall, at Disclosing Party's request, return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information and all other tangible materials and devices provided to the Receiving Party as Confidential Information, or at Disclosing Party's option, certify destruction of the same.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall, at Disclosing Party's request, return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information and all other tangible materials and devices provided to the Receiving Party as Confidential Information, or at Disclosing Party's option, certify destruction of the same, **unless the Receiving Party has invested substantial resources into using said information; in such cases, the Receiving Party may retain a copy for archival purposes, subject to continued confidentiality obligations.**
perturbation.explanation: This adds an exception allowing the Receiving Party to retain a copy if they've invested substantial resources, even if the Disclosing Party requests its return. This contradicts the original requirement for complete return or destruction and can lead to disputes over what constitutes 'substantial resources' and 'archival purposes.' It contradicts the Disclosing Party's right to have everything returned
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall, at Disclosing Party's request, return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information and all other tangible materials and devices provided to the Receiving Party as Confidential Information, or at Disclosing Party's option, certify destruction of the same, unless the Receiving Party has invested substantial resources into using said information; in such cases, the Receiving Party may retain a copy for archival purposes, subject to continued confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception allowing the Receiving Party to retain confidential information under certain conditions ('substantial resources,' 'archival purposes'). This directly contradicts the original text, which mandates the return or destruction of all confidential information at the Disclosing Party's request. The introduction of this exception creates a strong in-text contradiction because the Disclosing Party's right to complete return/destruction is now conditional, as per the explanation provided, aligning with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, where inconsistent obligations make enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties. None of the provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party, their agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by an authorized employee of Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties. **However, email correspondence between authorized representatives may be considered a valid amendment to this agreement if it clearly demonstrates mutual consent to a modification.** None of the provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party, their agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by an authorized employee of Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces a contradiction to the 'entire agreement' clause. The original text requires a formal written agreement for any modifications. The added sentence now allows for email correspondence to modify the agreement, undermining the formality and certainty of the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties. However, email correspondence between authorized representatives may be considered a valid amendment to this agreement if it clearly demonstrates mutual consent to a modification. None of the provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party, their agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by an authorized employee of Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that modifications must be made via a written agreement signed by both parties. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing email correspondence to serve as a valid amendment if it demonstrates mutual consent. This directly contradicts the original requirement for a formal written agreement, therefore creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' type.
